full_content:
S321 smart-poller daemon ran with dispatch disabled for ~8 hours, undetected

INCIDENT (historical): From session start of S321 (2026-04-29 ~09:30 UTC) until daemon restart at 17:48 UTC, the GT-KB smart-poller daemon ran with dispatch disabled. Audit log confirms run '09-29-55Z-4d849e' ran 1988 iterations with actionable_prime_count: 16 and zero dispatch_results entries — meaning no spawn was attempted despite pending work.

DISCOVERY: owner observation ('the poller did not trigger you... or Codex either').

ROOT CAUSE: platform-level governance gap. No formal spec captured the auto-trigger contract; bridge GO conditions never asserted dispatch; tests existed but were orphans (not in any executed lane); doctor check verified state but not behavior; Codex VERIFIED issued without spec coverage check.

RECOVERY: at 17:48 UTC the daemon was restarted (LastTaskResult=267009 suggests unclean exit-and-restart). The new daemon (run '17-48-07Z-34169f') launched with dispatch enabled per CLI default. At 17:48:08 it spawned 'claude -p' at PID 27064 — the first successful auto-trigger of the session.

LESSONS ENCODED:
- `DCL-SMART-POLLER-AUTO-TRIGGER-001` v2 (mechanism-agnostic supersession; 2026-05-09) formalizes the auto-trigger contract: the bridge dispatch mechanism MUST dispatch on actionable signature change.
- `ADR-SMART-POLLER-OWNER-OUT-OF-LOOP-001` v2 (mechanism-agnostic supersession; 2026-05-09) makes the architectural decision substrate-independent.
- `DCL-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-MANDATORY-001` captures the meta-lesson (mechanical verification beats narrative claim).
- The platform spec-coverage architecture (bridge `gtkb-platform-spec-coverage-architecture-2026-04-29-003.md`) prevents this class across all GT-KB-adopter projects.

POST-SLICE-4 DETECTOR (v2 reframing): the smart poller daemon was retired in Slice 4 (2026-05-09); the equivalent failure mode in the cross-harness event-driven trigger substrate is detected by `_check_cross_harness_trigger` (PASS/WARN/FAIL covering trigger script presence, both PostToolUse + Stop hook registrations, and dispatch-state freshness) and `_check_bridge_dispatch_liveness` (per-recipient `updated_at` freshness from `dispatch-state.json`). A v1-class incident in the new substrate would surface as an ALARM in `_check_bridge_dispatch_liveness` for either recipient with stale `updated_at`. The detector contract is byte-equivalent in semantics to what the smart-poller doctor check would have caught had the auto-trigger contract been formalized.

SUPERSEDES: v1 (2026-04-29) named the smart-poller doctor check as the desired enhancement. v2 (2026-05-09) reframes for the post-Slice-4 substrate: the cross-harness-trigger doctor checks are the live detectors; the smart-poller substrate is archived.

[
  {
    "id": "PB-INCIDENT-S321-DAEMON-DISPATCH-DISABLED-001.A1",
    "kind": "behavioral",
    "description": "The cross-harness event-driven trigger MUST dispatch on actionable signature change. The detector `_check_cross_harness_trigger` MUST report PASS/WARN/FAIL covering trigger script presence, both PostToolUse + Stop hook registrations (Claude + Codex), and dispatch-state freshness; `_check_bridge_dispatch_liveness` MUST report ALARM when a recipient's `updated_at` is stale beyond the threshold while INDEX has actionable work for that recipient.",
    "verifying_test": "groundtruth-kb/tests/test_doctor_cross_harness_trigger.py + groundtruth-kb/tests/test_doctor_bridge_dispatch_liveness.py"
  }
]
explicit_change_request: Approve as drafted (D5c item 4 AskUserQuestion answer 2026-05-09): user selected 'Approve as drafted' for v2 of PB-INCIDENT-S321-DAEMON-DISPATCH-DISABLED-001. Title preserved. Description preserves historical incident, reframes lessons to cite v2 ADR/DCL supersessions, adds POST-SLICE-4 DETECTOR section, adds NEW assertion A1.
change_reason: Slice 4 D5c item 4 (v2 supersession): incident history preserved; lessons encoded reframed for post-Slice-4 cross-harness-trigger doctor detectors. Authority: bridge gtkb-bridge-poller-event-driven-replacement-slice-4-smart-poller-retirement-001 GO at -016.
